headline: Is Broadcom (AVGO) Pricing Reflect Multi Year Gains And Recent Share Price Pullback
summary: If you are wondering whether Broadcom's current share price really lines up with its underlying value, you are not alone. This article is designed to help you frame that question clearly. The stock last closed at US$313.84, with returns of a 3.6% decline over 7 days, a 5.3% decline over 30 days, a 9.7% decline year to date, 68.9% over 1 year, a very large gain over 3 years and close to 7x over 5 years. This raises fair questions about what is already reflected in the price. Recent coverage...

headline: Nvidia Swears Off an Earnings Crutch, Putting Pressure on Other Tech Companies
summary: Nvidia  took longer to figure this out than it should have, but at least now it is taking the high road.  The chip maker last week said it would no longer exclude stock-based compensation expense from its nonstandard financial metrics, starting with the current quarter.  Nvidia’s decision is a welcome move, and means the adjusted profits that it highlights will be more in line with the results it reports under GAAP accounting.

content.title: Nvidia Swears Off an Earnings Crutch, Putting Pressure on Other Tech Companies
content.summary: Nvidia  took longer to figure this out than it should have, but at least now it is taking the high road.  The chip maker last week said it would no longer exclude stock-based compensation expense from its nonstandard financial metrics, starting with the current quarter.  Nvidia’s decision is a welcome move, and means the adjusted profits that it highlights will be more in line with the results it reports under GAAP accounting.

headline: Broadcom Earnings Are the Latest to Try to Climb AI Wall of Fear
summary: The chipmaker’s shares are down 24% from a December record, well underperforming the S&P 500 Index.  The selloff is part of investors’ broader rotation away from the largest technology companies due to fears about the sustainability of the hundreds of billions of dollars committed to developing artificial intelligence capabilities.  Broadcom, which is the seventh-most valuable company in the S&P 500 at $1.5 trillion, is a chipmaking partner with Alphabet Inc. and other AI giants, making it a beneficiary of that largesse.

content.title: Broadcom Earnings Are the Latest to Try to Climb AI Wall of Fear

The selloff is part of investors’ broader rotation away from the largest technology companies due to fears about the sustainability of the hundreds of billions of dollars committed to developing artificial intelligence capabilities.  Broadcom, which is the seventh-most valuable company in the S&P 500 at $1.5 trillion, is a chipmaking partner with Alphabet Inc. and other AI giants, making it a beneficiary of that largesse.

headline: Broadcom forecasts second-quarter revenue above estimates
summary: Chip designer Broadcom forecast second-quarter revenue above Wall Street estimates on ‌Wednesday, a sign of strong demand for ‌advanced chips used in data centers powering artificial intelligence applications.  The ​company expects quarterly revenue to be about $22.0 billion, above analysts' average estimate of $20.56 billion, according to data compiled by LSEG.  "Our AI revenue growth is ‌accelerating, and we ⁠expect AI semiconductor revenue to be $10.7 billion in Q2," CEO Hock Tan said ⁠in a statement.

content.title: Broadcom forecasts second-quarter revenue above estimates
content.summary: Chip designer Broadcom forecast second-quarter revenue above Wall Street estimates on ‌Wednesday, a sign of strong demand for ‌advanced chips used in data centers powering artificial intelligence applications.  The ​company expects quarterly revenue to be about $22.0 billion, above analysts' average estimate of $20.56 billion, according to data compiled by LSEG.  "Our AI revenue growth is ‌accelerating, and we ⁠expect AI semiconductor revenue to be $10.7 billion in Q2," CEO Hock Tan said ⁠in a statement.

headline: Broadcom earnings underwhelm investors as ROI concerns persist
summary: Broadcom (AVGO) reported first quarter results that narrowly beat analyst estimates on Wednesday after the closing bell. Adjusted earnings per share (EPS) came in at $2.05 (compared to analyst estimates of $2.03), while adjusted net revenue came in at $19.31 billion (compared to analyst estimates of $19.26 billion). The company forecasts second quarter revenue of $22 billion, topping Wall Street's $20.53 billion estimate. Market Domination Overtime host Josh Lipton goes over the breaking numbers and chats with Creative Strategies CEO and principal analyst Ben Bajarin about his take on the earnings print, why the stock is flat after-hours, and what Broadcom investors need to see from the company. To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination Overtime.

content.title: Broadcom earnings underwhelm investors as ROI concerns persist
content.description:
Broadcom (AVGO) reported first quarter results that narrowly beat analyst estimates on Wednesday after the closing bell. Adjusted earnings per share (EPS) came in at $2.05 (compared to analyst estimates of $2.03), while adjusted net revenue came in at $19.31 billion (compared to analyst estimates of $19.26 billion).

The company forecasts second quarter revenue of $22 billion, topping Wall Street's $20.53 billion estimate.

Market Domination Overtime host Josh Lipton goes over the breaking numbers and chats with Creative Strategies CEO and principal analyst Ben Bajarin about his take on the earnings print, why the stock is flat after-hours, and what Broadcom investors need to see from the company.

To watch more expert insights and analysis on the latest market action, check out more Market Domination Overtime.

headline: Broadcom’s (NASDAQ:AVGO) Q4 Sales Top Estimates, Inventory Levels Improve
summary: Fabless chip and software maker Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) reported Q4 CY2025 results exceeding the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 29.5% year on year to $19.31 billion. On top of that, next quarter’s revenue guidance ($22 billion at the midpoint) was surprisingly good and 7.4% above what analysts were expecting. Its non-GAAP profit of $2.05 per share was 1.3% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

content.title: Broadcom’s (NASDAQ:AVGO) Q4 Sales Top Estimates, Inventory Levels Improve
content.summary: Fabless chip and software maker Broadcom (NASDAQ:AVGO) reported Q4 CY2025 results exceeding the market’s revenue expectations, with sales up 29.5% year on year to $19.31 billion. On top of that, next quarter’s revenue guidance ($22 billion at the midpoint) was surprisingly good and 7.4% above what analysts were expecting. Its non-GAAP profit of $2.05 per share was 1.3% above analysts’ consensus estimates.

headline: Broadcom sees revenue above estimates as AI fuels custom chip demand
summary: Chip designer Broadcom forecast second-quarter revenue above Wall Street estimates on Wednesday, a sign of strong demand for advanced chips used in data centers ‌powering artificial intelligence applications.  Big Tech firms such as Alphabet, Microsoft, Amazon and Meta are expected to spend at least $630 billion ​to build AI infrastructure this year, which would boost demand for chips, servers, storage and networking equipment from companies like Broadcom.  "Our AI revenue growth is accelerating, and we expect AI semiconductor revenue to be $10.7 billion in Q2," CEO Hock Tan said in a statement.

headline: Broadcom Logs Higher Sales, Authorizes $10 Billion Buyback as AI Demand Climbs
summary: Broadcom  posted higher revenue and authorized a share buyback as AI demand continues to drive sales.  The semiconductor and software maker on Wednesday posted a first-quarter profit of $7.35 billion, or $1.50 a share, compared with $5.50 billion, or $1.14 a share, a year earlier.  Stripping out certain one-time items, adjusted per-share earnings were $2.05, ahead of the $2.03 anticipated by analysts, according to FactSet.

content.title: Broadcom Logs Higher Sales, Authorizes $10 Billion Buyback as AI Demand Climbs
content.summary: Broadcom  posted higher revenue and authorized a share buyback as AI demand continues to drive sales.  The semiconductor and software maker on Wednesday posted a first-quarter profit of $7.35 billion, or $1.50 a share, compared with $5.50 billion, or $1.14 a share, a year earlier.  Stripping out certain one-time items, adjusted per-share earnings were $2.05, ahead of the $2.03 anticipated by analysts, according to FactSet.
